marketState: neutral — hot near-term weather and a fair-value discount offset by a storage surplus and uniformly bearish technicals
summary: Seasonality data (vsMedian, probHigher, typicalMove4w, reliability) is entirely null in this snapshot, so the single highest-priority input for this dashboard is unavailable and conviction is capped accordingly. What remains is genuinely mixed: 8-16D weather demand is +31.4% vs the 5y norm and printing above its historical max, a clearly bullish cooling-demand signal, and front price ($2.889) sits only ~1% under a rising fair-value curve ($2.917 now, drifting to $3.034 by +21D), which is mildly supportive. Against that, storage is +184 Bcf above the 5y average with a +72 Bcf bearish build surprise, and every technical timeframe from 5m to 1w shows a bearish trend with a 50<200 EMA cross, though RSI is mid-range rather than oversold. COT managed money is net short (-60,295) but only at the 35th percentile of its 5y range, so positioning is not extreme in either direction. With the top-priority seasonality signal missing and the remaining evidence roughly balanced, this reads as a no-edge environment rather than a clean directional setup.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonality — the top-priority input for this horizon — is completely empty in this snapshot, so there is no base-rate or typical-move read to anchor a multi-week trade. The remaining evidence pulls in opposite directions: 8-16D weather demand above its historical max and a front price trading under a rising fair-value curve are bullish, while storage sitting +184 Bcf above the 5y average with a bearish +72 Bcf surprise is a real supply overhang, and mild net-short COT positioning (35th percentile) adds no strong tilt either way.
longTerm.invalidation: n/a — no position held; would revisit if seasonality data repopulates or storage surprises reverse to bullish (smaller-than-forecast builds) for two consecutive reports
longTerm.trigger: Wait for seasonality metrics (vsMedian, probHigher, typicalMove4w) to repopulate. If they confirm the weather/fair-value bullish tilt and a storage report shows a build smaller than forecast, favor LONG toward the $3.03-3.53 plus21D fair-value band. If instead storage keeps printing large bearish surprises while weather demand rolls back toward normal, favor SHORT toward the $2.42-2.47 band low.

shortTerm.rationale: Front price is trading essentially at fair value (spread only -$0.028, status FAIR) so there is no valuation edge to lean on. Near-term weather demand is elevated (+20% vs 5y) but still inside its normal range (rangePos 0.82, not above max), while every technical timeframe from 5m to 1w shows a bearish trend with a 50<200 EMA cross yet RSI (34.5-50.7) is not oversold, and the futures curve shows only a marginal -$0.03 backwardation front-to-next — none of this is decisive enough to size a 1-10 day trade.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either (a) RSI basing under 30 with a 50-EMA reclaim on the 1h/4h to confirm a technical low worth buying into the hot weather, or (b) a clean daily close below the ~$2.42-2.45 fair-value band low to confirm the bearish technical structure is taking over and sell the breakdown.
